title: Kangaroo Level 3-4 - Practice Set 4: Mixed Topics
disclaimer: Original practice problems inspired by Math Kangaroo format. Not official AKSF content.

questions.question_text: Mia has 27 marbles. She gives 8 to her brother Leo and then finds 5 more on the playground. How many marbles does Mia have now?
questions.solution: Start with 27. Subtract 8 given away: 27 - 8 = 19. Add 5 found: 19 + 5 = 24.

questions.question_text: Tom draws the shape shown below by combining identical small squares. How many small squares did Tom use in total?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 120 120' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='10' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='40' y='10' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='10' y='40' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='40' y='40' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='70' y='40' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='40' y='70' width='30' height='30'/></svg>
questions.image_description: An L-shaped figure made of 6 small squares arranged in a staircase pattern.
questions.solution: Count each unit square in the figure: 2 on the top row, 3 in the middle row, 1 on the bottom. Total = 2 + 3 + 1 = 6.

questions.question_text: Anna's piano lesson starts at the time shown on the clock below and lasts for 40 minutes. At what time does the lesson end?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 100 100' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke-width='2'><circle cx='50' cy='50' r='40'/><line x1='50' y1='50' x2='50' y2='20'/><line x1='50' y1='50' x2='75' y2='62'/><circle cx='50' cy='50' r='2' fill='currentColor'/></svg>
questions.image_description: An analog clock showing 3:45 (hour hand between 3 and 4, minute hand on 9).
questions.solution: Add 40 minutes to 3:45. 3:45 + 15 minutes = 4:00. Then add the remaining 25 minutes: 4:00 + 25 = 4:25 PM.

questions.question_text: In the pattern 2, 5, 8, 11, 14, ..., each number is 3 more than the one before. What is the 7th number?
Ben buys a notebook for $4 and two pencils for $1 each, as shown. He pays with a $10 bill. How much change does Ben receive?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 160 60' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='10' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='70' y='15' width='10' height='35'/><rect x='90' y='15' width='10' height='35'/><text x='30' y='35' font-size='12' fill='currentColor' text-anchor='middle' stroke='none'>$4</text><text x='75' y='60' font-size='8' fill='currentColor' text-anchor='middle' stroke='none'>$1</text><text x='95' y='60' font-size='8' fill='currentColor' text-anchor='middle' stroke='none'>$1</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A notebook labeled $4 next to two pencils each labeled $1.
questions.solution: Total cost = 4 + 1 + 1 = 6 dollars. Change = 10 - 6 = 4 dollars.

questions.question_text: Lily draws the arrow shape below. How many right angles (square corners) does the arrow have along its outline?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 140 100' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke-width='2'><polygon points='10,40 70,40 70,20 120,50 70,80 70,60 10,60'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A horizontal right-pointing arrow with a rectangular tail and a triangular head.
questions.solution: The arrow outline has right angles at the back-top corner, back-bottom corner, the two notches where the rectangle meets the head, and the step corners at the tail. Counting each 90-degree corner gives 6.

questions.question_text: A basket holds 12 apples. A farmer fills 5 such baskets completely. How many apples are there in total?
questions.solution: 5 baskets x 12 apples = 60 apples.

questions.question_text: Three friends Pam, Quinn, and Ravi line up. Pam is not first. Ravi is last. Who is first in line?
questions.solution: Ravi is last, so Ravi is 3rd. Pam is not first, so Pam is 2nd. Therefore Quinn is 1st.

questions.question_text: Sara thinks of a number. She doubles it, then adds 7, and gets 25. What number did Sara think of?
questions.solution: If 2n + 7 = 25, then 2n = 18, so n = 9.

questions.question_text: Carl folds a square piece of paper in half along the dashed line shown. What shape does he get after the fold?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 100 100' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='10' width='80' height='80'/><line x1='50' y1='10' x2='50' y2='90' stroke-dasharray='4 4'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A square with a vertical dashed line through its center.
questions.solution: Folding a square in half along a line connecting midpoints of opposite sides produces a rectangle that is half as wide as the original square.

questions.question_text: A pizza is cut into 8 equal slices. Diego eats 3 slices and Emma eats 2 slices. What fraction of the pizza is left?
questions.image_description: A circle divided into 8 equal slices by 4 lines through the center.
questions.solution: Slices eaten: 3 + 2 = 5. Slices left: 8 - 5 = 3. Fraction left = 3/8.

questions.question_text: A ribbon is 120 cm long. Nina cuts it into 4 equal pieces. How long is each piece?
questions.solution: Divide the length by 4: 120 / 4 = 30 cm.

questions.question_text: At Rosa's party there are 4 more girls than boys. If there are 14 children in total, how many boys are at the party?
questions.solution: If boys = b, girls = b + 4. Total: b + b + 4 = 14, so 2b = 10, b = 5.

questions.question_text: Jack stacks blocks in a pattern: row 1 has 1 block, row 2 has 3 blocks, row 3 has 5 blocks, row 4 has 7 blocks. How many blocks are in row 6?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 160 120' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke-width='2'><rect x='70' y='10' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='50' y='30' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='70' y='30' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='90' y='30' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='30' y='50' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='50' y='50' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='70' y='50' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='90' y='50' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='110' y='50' width='20' height='20'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A triangular stack of blocks showing 1 block on top, 3 blocks in the second row, and 5 blocks in the third row.
questions.solution: The pattern adds 2 each row: 1, 3, 5, 7, 9, 11. Row 6 has 11 blocks.

questions.question_text: A pencil costs $2 and an eraser costs $1. Zoe buys 3 pencils and some erasers and pays $10 in total. How many erasers did Zoe buy?
questions.solution: Pencils cost 3 x 2 = 6 dollars. Remaining for erasers: 10 - 6 = 4 dollars. At $1 each, that is 4 erasers.

questions.question_text: Oliver arranges toothpicks to form the figure below with identical small triangles. How many small triangles are in the figure?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 120 80' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke-width='2'><polygon points='10,70 40,70 25,45'/><polygon points='40,70 70,70 55,45'/><polygon points='70,70 100,70 85,45'/><polygon points='25,45 55,45 40,70'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Three upward triangles in a row sharing bases, with one downward triangle between the first two.
questions.solution: Count the triangles: 3 upward-pointing and 1 downward-pointing = 4 small triangles.

questions.question_text: Four children Ava, Ben, Cleo, and Dan compare heights. Ava is taller than Ben. Cleo is shorter than Ben. Dan is taller than Ava. Who is the shortest?
questions.solution: From the clues: Dan > Ava > Ben > Cleo. So Cleo is the shortest.

questions.question_text: The digits 1, 2, 3, and 4 are each used exactly once to form two two-digit numbers. What is the largest possible sum of these two numbers?
questions.solution: The sum is 10(a + b) + (c + d) where {a, b, c, d} = {1, 2, 3, 4}. To maximize, put the two largest digits (4 and 3) in the tens places: 10 x 7 = 70. Add the remaining digits in the ones places: 1 + 2 = 3. Maximum sum = 70 + 3 = 73. For example, 41 + 32 = 73.

questions.question_text: The shape below is made of unit squares. What is the perimeter of the shape (in units)?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 120 120' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='10' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='40' y='10' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='40' y='40' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='70' y='40' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='40' y='70' width='30' height='30'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A zigzag shape made of 5 unit squares.
questions.solution: Tracing the outline unit by unit around the 5-square staircase gives 12 unit segments on the boundary, so the perimeter is 12.

questions.question_text: Mia's movie starts at 6:50 PM and lasts 1 hour and 35 minutes. At what time does the movie end?
questions.solution: 6:50 + 1 hour = 7:50. Then 7:50 + 10 minutes = 8:00, and 8:00 + 25 minutes = 8:25 PM.

questions.question_text: Kiran writes down the numbers 1, 2, 3, ... up to 30. How many times does the digit 2 appear in total?
questions.solution: Ones-place 2s: 2, 12, 22 -> 3 times. Tens-place 2s: 20 through 29 -> 10 times. Total = 3 + 10 = 13.

questions.question_text: Five cards labeled A, B, C, D, E are placed in a row. A is at the left end. B is directly to the right of A. C is directly to the right of B. D is directly to the right of C. Which card is in the middle (position 3)?
questions.solution: From the clues the order is A, B, C, D, E. The card in position 3 (the middle) is C.

questions.question_text: A 3x3x3 cube has all 6 outer faces painted red. Then the cube is cut into 27 identical small cubes. How many small cubes have exactly 2 red faces?
questions.image_description: A 3D view of a large cube showing its top, front, and right faces.
questions.solution: Small cubes with exactly 2 painted faces sit on the edges of the big cube but are not corners. A cube has 12 edges, and each edge of a 3x3x3 cube contributes 1 such middle small cube. Total = 12.

questions.question_text: Eli writes a two-digit number. He then swaps the tens digit and the ones digit, and the new number is 27 larger than the original. Which of these could be Eli's original number?
questions.solution: For a two-digit number with tens t and ones u, swapping gives (10u + t) - (10t + u) = 9(u - t) = 27, so u - t = 3. Checking 14: u - t = 4 - 1 = 3. Check: 41 - 14 = 27. So 14 works.
